[Albert V, Duke of Bavaria](/wiki/Albert_V,_Duke_of_Bavaria "Albert V, Duke of Bavaria")

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Ernest+of+Bavaria%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Ernest+of+Bavaria%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Ernest+of+Bavaria%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Ernest+of+Bavaria%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(October 2018)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

([German](/wiki/German_language "German language"): *Ernst von Bayern*) (17 December 1554 – 17 February 1612) was [Prince-Elector](/wiki/Prince-Elector "Prince-Elector")-[Archbishop](/wiki/Archbishop "Archbishop") of the [Archbishopric of Cologne](/wiki/Electorate_of_Cologne "Electorate of Cologne") and, as such, [Archchancellor](/wiki/Archchancellor "Archchancellor") of the [Holy Roman Empire](/wiki/Holy_Roman_Empire "Holy Roman Empire") and [Duke of Westphalia](/wiki/Duchy_of_Westphalia "Duchy of Westphalia"), from 1583 to 1612 as successor of the expelled Archbishop [Gebhard Truchsess von Waldburg](/wiki/Gebhard_Truchsess_von_Waldburg "Gebhard Truchsess von Waldburg").

*Werewolf histories* (First published 2015 ed.). Basingstoke, Hampshire New York, NY: Palgrave Macmillan. 2015. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [9781137526342](/wiki/Special:BookSources/9781137526342 "Special:BookSources/9781137526342").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Prince-Elector-Archbishop of Cologne from 1583 to 1612

This article is about the Archbishop of Cologne. For the earlier Bavarian ruler named Ernest, see [Ernest, Duke of Bavaria](/wiki/Ernest,_Duke_of_Bavaria "Ernest, Duke of Bavaria"). For other people named Ernest of Bavaria, see [Ernest of Bavaria (disambiguation)](/wiki/Ernest_of_Bavaria_(disambiguation) "Ernest of Bavaria (disambiguation)").

| --- | --- |

Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Ernest of Bavaria"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Ernest+of+Bavaria%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Ernest+of+Bavaria%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Ernest+of+Bavaria%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Ernest+of+Bavaria%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Ernest+of+Bavaria%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Ernest+of+Bavaria%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(October 2018)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[Wittelsbach](/wiki/House_of_Wittelsbach "House of Wittelsbach")-[Hapsburg](/wiki/House_of_Habsburg "House of Habsburg") aristocrat **Ernest of Bavaria** ([German](/wiki/German_language "German language"): *Ernst von Bayern*) (17 December 1554 – 17 February 1612) was [Prince-Elector](/wiki/Prince-Elector "Prince-Elector")-[Archbishop](/wiki/Archbishop "Archbishop") of the [Archbishopric of Cologne](/wiki/Electorate_of_Cologne "Electorate of Cologne") and, as such, [Archchancellor](/wiki/Archchancellor "Archchancellor") of the [Holy Roman Empire](/wiki/Holy_Roman_Empire "Holy Roman Empire") and [Duke of Westphalia](/wiki/Duchy_of_Westphalia "Duchy of Westphalia"), from 1583 to 1612 as successor of the expelled Archbishop [Gebhard Truchsess von Waldburg](/wiki/Gebhard_Truchsess_von_Waldburg "Gebhard Truchsess von Waldburg").

He was also simultaneously [Prince-Bishop](/wiki/Prince-Bishop "Prince-Bishop") of [Münster](/wiki/Bishopric_of_M%C3%BCnster "Bishopric of Münster"), [Hildesheim](/wiki/Bishopric_of_Hildesheim "Bishopric of Hildesheim"), [Freising](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Archdiocese_of_Munich "Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Munich"), and [Liège](/wiki/Bishopric_of_Li%C3%A8ge "Bishopric of Liège"), [Count of Loon](/wiki/County_of_Loon "County of Loon"), and [Prince-Abbot of Stavelot](/wiki/Princely_Abbey_of_Stavelot-Malmedy "Princely Abbey of Stavelot-Malmedy"). In Freising he had already become bishop by the age of 12.

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ernest_of_Bavaria&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Ernest was born in [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), the son of [Albert V, Duke of Bavaria](/wiki/Albert_V,_Duke_of_Bavaria "Albert V, Duke of Bavaria"), and [Anna of Austria](/wiki/Anna_of_Austria_(1528%E2%80%931590) "Anna of Austria (1528–1590)"). Duke Albert had destined his third son, Ernest, for the clerical vocation. He was educated and trained by the [Jesuits](/wiki/Jesuits "Jesuits") at [Ingolstadt](/wiki/Ingolstadt "Ingolstadt").[[1]](#cite_note-1): 168
